AN NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN THE CITY OF EDEN. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ROUGHLY 6 MINUTES BEFORE LIFTING AS IT APPROACHED THE VIRGINIA AND NORTH CAROLINA STATE LINE, PRODUCING DAMAGE TO TREES AND STRUCTURES.

ADDITIONAL MUDSLIDES AND ROAD CLOSURES DUE TO CREEK FLOODING HAVE ALSO BEEN REPORTED. FLOODING CONTINUES ACROSS MUCH OF THE AREA.

*** 1 INJ *** TORNADO WITH UP TO EF2 LEVEL DAMAGE CROSSED OUT OF DAVIE COUNTY,NC INTO YADKIN COUNTY,NC SOUTHWEST OF BAITY ROAD AROUND 417 PM EDT, AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST AT AROUND 40 MPH ON A CONTINUOUS PATH FOR 4.1 MILES BEFORE LIFTING AND DISSIPATING JUST NORTHEAST OF WATKINS ROAD AROUND 423 PM EDT. APPROXIMATELY 45 HOMES AND BUILDINGS ALONG THE TORNADIC PATH AT LEAST PARTIALLY DAMAGED.
SEVERAL OBSERVED SIGHTINGS OF A TORNADO...COURTNEY ELEMENTARY SCHOOL GYMNASIUM DESTROYED...SEVERAL VEHICLES FLIPPED...AND PEOPLE TRAPPED INSIDE HOMES

NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND SIX TO SEVEN HOMES WERE DAMAGED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS IN THE COMMUNITY OF HAMPTONVILLE.

A NWS DAMAGE ASSESSMENT TEAM HAS CONCLUDED THAT A TORNADO IMPACTED PORTIONS OF STOKES COUNTY DURING THE LATE AFTERNOON AND EARLY EVENING OF MAY 24, 2017. THE TORNADO REACHED A MAXIMUM INTENSITY OF EF-2, PRODUCED WIND SPEEDS AS HIGH AS 125MPH, AND DAMAGED OR DESTROYED NUMEROUS MOBILE HOMES, CABINS, OUTBUILDINGS, AND TREES.NUMBER OF DAMAGED STRUCTURES IS ESTIMATED TO BE NEAR 100. THE GREATEST DAMAGE WAS NOTED NEAR SAURATOWN MOUNTAIN IN THE YMCA CAMP HANES, WHERE PATH WIDTH WAS MEASURED AT 0.5 MILE
